overall_summary: Big gap to reference, but the data shows exactly where it is hiding. Attack these points. Current gap: 30.173s slower than reference. The biggest opportunity is Sector 1 (+14.984s), where 80.2 km/h is being left on the table through the corners. Executing these fixes could recover 3.16s — a completely different lap time.

priority_actions.issue: Apex speed is 70.7 km/h below reference (23.7 vs 94.4 km/h). Significant corner exit speed deficit.
priority_actions.instruction: Despite a late brake point, apex speed is below reference — car not rotating. Use higher initial brake pressure to pitch the car in, trail off through apex. Hit 94 km/h and the exit speed takes care of itself.
priority_actions.evidence: Apex: 23.7 km/h vs 94.4 km/h reference (-70.7 km/h).

priority_actions.issue: Entry speed is 53.1 km/h below reference but apex speed is acceptable — over-braking on entry.
priority_actions.instruction: Trail the brakes into the corner rather than releasing fully at turn-in. Carry more entry speed and use trail braking to rotate the car to the apex.

priority_actions.issue: Apex speed is 16.9 km/h below reference (37.5 vs 54.4 km/h). Significant corner exit speed deficit.
priority_actions.instruction: Despite a late brake point, apex speed is below reference — car not rotating. Use higher initial brake pressure to pitch the car in, trail off through apex. Hit 54 km/h and the exit speed takes care of itself.
priority_actions.evidence: Apex: 37.5 km/h vs 54.4 km/h reference (-16.9 km/h).

priority_actions.issue: Throttle pick-up is 20m later than reference after the apex.
priority_actions.instruction: Get back to full throttle 20m earlier. Reference applies throttle at 2710m, driver at 2730m. Trust the apex and commit — late throttle bleeds straight-line speed.

sector_feedback.headline: Minimum corner speed is 80.2 km/h below reference — driver is over-slowing significantly through this sector.
Minimum corner speed is 16.9 km/h below reference — driver is over-slowing significantly through this sector.
sector_feedback.details: Throttle commitment is strong — 14% higher average than reference.
